skills.name: Rational Agents
skills.description: Agents that select actions to maximize expected utility given their percept history and environment model.

skills.name: State-Space Search
skills.description: Formulating problems as a graph of states connected by actions with step costs.

skills.name: Breadth-First Search
skills.description: Uninformed search that expands the shallowest unexpanded node. Complete and optimal for uniform step cost.

skills.name: Depth-First Search
skills.description: Uninformed search that expands the deepest unexpanded node. Space-efficient but not optimal.

skills.name: Uniform-Cost Search
skills.description: Expands the node with lowest path cost g(n). Optimal for non-negative step costs.

skills.name: Heuristics
skills.description: Functions h(n) estimating cost from n to goal. Admissible if never overestimates; consistent if monotone.

skills.name: A* Search
skills.description: Informed best-first search using f(n) = g(n) + h(n). Optimal when h is admissible.

skills.name: Constraint Satisfaction Problems
skills.description: Problems defined by variables, domains, and constraints; solutions assign values satisfying all constraints.

skills.name: Backtracking Search
skills.description: Depth-first CSP solver that assigns one variable at a time, backtracking on dead ends.

skills.name: Arc Consistency (AC-3)
skills.description: Constraint propagation that enforces every pair (Xi, Xj) has consistent values.

skills.name: Local Search
skills.description: Hill climbing, simulated annealing, and genetic algorithms over complete-state formulations.

skills.name: Minimax
skills.description: Recursive adversarial game-tree search assuming optimal opponent.

skills.name: Alpha-Beta Pruning
skills.description: Minimax optimization that skips branches that cannot affect the root value.

skills.name: Expectimax
skills.description: Game-tree search with chance nodes; replaces min with expected value.

skills.name: Utility Theory
skills.description: Rational preferences satisfying axioms are equivalent to maximizing expected utility.

skills.name: Markov Decision Processes
skills.description: Sequential decisions with states, actions, transitions T(s,a,s'), rewards R, and discount gamma.

skills.name: Bellman Equations
skills.description: Recursive definitions of V*(s) and Q*(s,a) for optimal policies in MDPs.

skills.name: Value Iteration
skills.description: Iterative algorithm computing V* by repeatedly applying the Bellman update.

skills.name: Policy Iteration
skills.description: Alternate policy evaluation and policy improvement until convergence.

skills.name: Temporal Difference Learning
skills.description: Model-free value learning from samples using TD targets.

skills.name: Q-Learning
skills.description: Off-policy TD method learning Q*(s,a) directly; converges under mild conditions.

skills.name: Exploration vs Exploitation
skills.description: Trade-off between trying new actions (ε-greedy, UCB) and exploiting best-known.

skills.name: Probability Basics
skills.description: Joint / conditional / marginal distributions and the product and sum rules.

skills.name: Bayes' Rule
skills.description: P(A|B) = P(B|A) P(A) / P(B). Foundation of probabilistic inference.

skills.name: Bayes Nets
skills.description: DAG representation of joint distributions via conditional independencies.

skills.name: Variable Elimination
skills.description: Exact inference algorithm that marginalizes variables one at a time using factors.

skills.name: Sampling Inference
skills.description: Prior sampling, rejection sampling, likelihood weighting, and Gibbs sampling.

skills.name: Hidden Markov Models
skills.description: Temporal Bayes nets with hidden state sequence and observations.

skills.name: Particle Filtering
skills.description: Approximate HMM inference with sample-based representation of belief state.
